title: cirsium bulbosum subsp. anglicum
intro: Cirsium bulbosum subsp. anglicum, also known as the English thistle or Scotch thistle, is a biennial plant belonging to the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cirsium bulbosum subsp. anglicum include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, although its use should be limited to small amounts and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
